session.assistant: So `create_plan_issue_block` is an alias for `create_plan_block`, which uses `plan_number`. Let me also check what key the block data uses.
session.tool_result: 238→def create_plan_block( 239→ plan_number: int, 240→ worktree_name: str, 241→ timestamp: str, 242→ plan_file: str | None = None, 243→) -> MetadataBlock: 244→ """Create an erk-plan block with validation. 245→ 246→ Args: 247→ plan_number: GitHub plan number for this plan 248→ worktree_name: Auto-generated worktree name from issue title 249→ timestamp: ISO 8601 timestamp of issue creation 250→ plan_file: Optional path to the plan file 251→ 252→ Returns: 253→ MetadataBlock with erk-plan schema 254→ """ 255→ schema = PlanSchema() 256→ data: dict[str, Any] = { 257→ "plan_number": plan_number, 258→ "worktree_name": worktree_name, 259→ "timestamp": timestamp, 260→ } 261→ 262→ if plan_file is not None: 263→ data["plan_file"] = plan_file 264→ 265→ return create_metadata_block( 266→ key=schema.get_key(), 267→ data=data,
session.assistant: The source stores `"plan_number"` as the dict key (line 257), not `"pr_number"`. So line 79 in the test (`block.data["pr_number"]`) is also wrong. Let me fix the whole file.
